characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The Leader: He's the one who calls the shots. No Name Given: The toy bios of all the other Joes give out their full name, while Lt. Stone's name is only given as "Stone".

characters.name: Lt. Stone
characters.description: The Leader: He's the one who calls the shots. No Name Given: The toy bios of all the other Joes give out their full name, while Lt. Stone's name is only given as "Stone".

characters.name: Sgt. Savage
characters.description: Fish out of Temporal Water: He's a soldier from the 1940's now living in the show's current time. Human Popsicle: He was frozen until he thawed out in today's time. Number Two: To Stone; he takes charge in Stone's absence.

characters.name: Black Dragon
characters.description: Expy: While the Extreme line based their characters on various Joes, Black Dragon is just blatantly Snake Eyes. They're even both blonde! The only difference is, Black Dragon is NOTThe Speechless. Race Lift: In a pretty spectacular manner, all things considered. He only appeared in one franchise and this happened to him. His bio establishes him as being Chinese, yet he has constantly been depicted as a blond-haired guy.

characters.name: Freight
characters.description: The Big Guy: He visibly towers over the rest of the Joes. Especially impressive given theHeroic Buildof his comrades. Dynamic Entry: His first appearance sees him hurling himself out of a Jet ontoSKAR'sbase, using only blaster fire to cushion his landing.

characters.name: Mayday
characters.description: Action Girl: She's an active member of the team who can fight with the best of them. Badass Driver: The team's most skilled pilot and vehicle operator. Her introduction even has her show off some of her skills to Lt. Stone as ordered by Mr. Clancy, the man introducing Stone's team. Deadpan Snarker: She's the most snarky and quippy of the Joes.Lt. Stone: "Here, take these with you. *hands Mayday a pair of crutches*"Mayday: "Oh yeah, thanks. I can work on my golf swings." The Smurfette Principle: She is the only female Joe in this series and the only recurring female character in the first season. Toyless Toyline Character: While plans were made, she never had a toy made for her.

characters.name: Metalhead
characters.description: Not to be confused with ARAH's Metal-Head. Hidden Depths: Looks and sounds like a stereotypical, well, metalhead. Heisone, but he's also a skilled technologist and soldier. Musical Assassin: Metalhead has a gun that plays rock music as a weapon. It blows up theMecha-Mookswith ease. The Power of Rock: He weaponizes rock music. The Mole: In the series finale, he pretends to turn traitor in order to help bring down SKAR.

characters.name: Quickstryke
characters.description: Heel–Face Turn: Was an agent of SKAR until his brother was killed by their experiments. My Nayme Is: His codename is spelled Quickstryke, rather than Quick Strike. Toyless Toyline Character: Like Mayday, there is no toy of him, but one was planned for an unreleased wave 2.

characters.name: Iron Klaw
characters.description: A ruthless masked terrorist in charge of the military wing of SKAR, he moonlights asCount von Raini, the leader of a small European nation. Skilled, manipulative and a master of disguise. Bad Boss: In the comic, he routinely kills his own troops, including for reasons as petty as someone accidentally touching him.Mean Boss: In the cartoon, he's not as actively malicious although he clearly cares little for the well-being of his troops. HisEstablishing Character Momentin the first episode is violently slapping down a SKAR soldier who accidentally called him Iron Klaw while he is still in his Count Von Rani guise. Mean Boss: In the cartoon, he's not as actively malicious although he clearly cares little for the well-being of his troops. HisEstablishing Character Momentin the first episode is violently slapping down a SKAR soldier who accidentally called him Iron Klaw while he is still in his Count Von Rani guise. Bastard Understudy: The Dark Horse comic reveals he assassinated his predecessor, the Duchess of Mklavia. Big Bad: He is the main antagonist, being the leader of SKAR and all.

characters.name: Inferno
characters.description: A pyromaniac with an irritating laugh and a bad sense of humor. Has a childlike mentality, regarding warfare as a game and the vehicles and weapons under his command as "toys". Ace Pilot: He's often seen piloting the Skystalker jet, and his Toy bios state his role in SKAR is "Jet Squadron leader", which is odd as there's nothing pilot-like about his design. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Is a recurring character in the first season of the cartoon but doesn't appear at all in the second, though a target dummy modeled after him does show up in "The Silencers" and he is mentioned by Rampage in "Wreckage: Revenge". Evil Has a Bad Sense of Humor: His first file card notes that he's a "notorious joker who's anything but funny".' "When is a runway no longer a runway? When it's BLOWN AWAY, ah-ha!''" Freudian Excuse: He was bullied by two rich kids when he was a child.

characters.name: Rampage
characters.description: An arms dealer who turns into a greenHulk-like monster when irritated, he supplies SKAR withMecha-Mooksand other weapons. He has sights on SKAR's leadership, but begrudgingly grovels under Iron Klaw's command until the right opportunity comes along. Berserk Button: Anything can aggravate Rampage if it escalates enough, but being bossed around will instantly set him off."NOBODY GIVES ME ORDERS!" Contrasting Sequel Antagonist: Rampage is in many ways similar to Destro fromG.I. Joe: A Real American Hero. Both are businessmen who supply robots and devices to the bad guys and are the most recurring villain next to the main antagonist, both wear some kind of armor withintegrated hand guns, and both are not afraid to voice their contempt for the main villain. However where Destro was cool and professional, Rampage is an unhinged, treacherous maniac. Depending on the Writer: Whether the gun on his shoulder works as a chaingun or a rocket launcher varies by episode. Large Ham: Yet another hammy villain. He especially hams it up when he's angry.

characters.name: Steel Raven
characters.description: The Dragon: She is Iron Klaw's second-in-command.

characters.name: Wreckage
characters.description: A soldier who formerly served with Freight. He was shot down during an unnamed war in South America and apparently into a grotesque cyborg by the Inter-Alliance. The Brute: A towering cyborg, he serves as Iron Klaw's field leader and attack dog. Cyborg: His right arm ends in a cybernetic hand that allows him to shoot lasers from his fingertips. Genius Bruiser: He's got both brawn and brains. Heel–Face Turn: In "Wreckage: Revenge", he finally learns that Iron Klaw is responsible for making him a cyborg and even contemplates joining the Joes at the end of the episode.
